mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits immediately through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is strongest when the policy rate is being cut from already-restrictive levels and fails when rates are stable or rising.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with a negative 1-month change in FEDFUNDS, or if the signal loses sign consistency during easing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a recent 3-month increase threshold signals deteriorating labor income and weaker consumption expectations, which pressures equities lower over the following month. The mechanism fails when unemployment rises but payroll losses are immediately offset by falling inflation and aggressive policy easing.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return is not materially below unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad demand weakening across consumption and housing. That joint contraction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter because earnings expectations and cyclical sentiment deteriorate together.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint decline in retail sales and housing starts does not outperform either series alone, or if 60d forward returns are not negative when both are below their 3-month trend.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO relative to a stable labor market signals improving production throughput and earnings leverage, supporting positive equity returns over the next month. The signal weakens if industrial output rises only because of inventory restocking without broad demand confirmation.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while PAYEMS is flat or positive over 1 month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show positive average 20d SPX returns, or if the signal disappears when PAYEMS is declining alongside INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is falling while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real policy becomes tighter and valuation pressure increases, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The claim fails if disinflation is matched by rapid rate cuts that offset the real-rate shock.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation trend turns down while FEDFUNDS stays above 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if negative real-rate pressure episodes do not precede below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the sign turns positive despite persistent high policy rates.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in GDPC1 over the latest quarter signals a broad demand recession that markets discount quickly, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when the GDP drop is not yet offset by aggressive monetary easing.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP contractions are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the signal is indistinguishable from unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS trend together with a falling UNRATE indicates labor market normalization and stronger household income, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when payroll growth is concentrated in low-wage sectors and unemployment does not improve.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases and UNRATE falls over the same 3-month window
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous payroll gains and unemployment declines do not precede positive 60d SPX returns, or if the joint condition performs no better than either variable alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST after a prior collapse marks stabilization in interest-sensitive demand and construction activity, which tends to support equities over the next week as cyclicals reprice upward. The signal fails when housing starts rise only from volatile weather effects or after mortgage conditions remain severely tight.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 5% month-over-month after a prior 2-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if the housing rebound trigger does not produce positive 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears when FEDFUNDS remains sharply restrictive.
